name: PROVECTUS BIOPHARMACEUTICALS, INC.
business: Provectus Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing immunotherapy medicines for different diseases based on bioactive, synthetic, small molecule rose bengal sodium. The company's drug product candidates are based on RBS and include clinical development programs in oncology (intratumoral administration), dermatology (topical), and ophthalmology (topical). The company has no prescription drug products approved for commercial sale and has never generated any substantial revenues.

exposures.sentence: Provectus Biopharmaceuticals, Inc., a Delaware corporation (together with its subsidiaries, Provectus or the Company), is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing immunotherapy medicines for different diseases.

catalysts.event: Initiation of intratumoral PV-10 monotherapy and combination therapy clinical trials in pre-operative penile SCC and FOLRINOX-refractory pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma metastatic to the liver
catalysts.sentence: The Company is planning to initiate new intratumoral PV-10 monotherapy and combination therapy clinical trials in pre-operative penile SCC and mPDAC indications to generate new clinical data and appropriately utilize historical clinical data from intratumoral PV-10 trials, EAPs, and/or QOL study of injectable solid tumor cancers.

catalysts.event: Filing and acceptance of an investigational new drug application with the FDA for a systemically administered formulation of PV-10
catalysts.sentence: Our goals, when this work is complete, are to file and have accepted an investigational new drug application (IND) with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), take an initial systemic drug product candidate into an early-stage clinic trial for an initial oncology or hematology indication, and/or pursue a co-development collaboration or out-license arrangement for this route of administration and disease area.

catalysts.event: Patent award from USPTO on August 5, 2025 for 'In vitro and xenograft anti-tumor activity of a halogenated-xanthene against refractory pediatric solid tumors'
